According to NS3.AI, the article explores the evolving legislative approach toward the crypto market. Uncertainty persists as stakeholders await further clarification and impact analysis.

data.title: Ethereum Launches $2M Quantum Defense Team as Threat Timeline Accelerates
data.description:
Ethereum Foundation elevates quantum resistance to top strategic priority with a $2 million Post Quantum team, launching prize competitions and multi-client development networks as industry experts warn quantum threats could materialize within years rather than decades.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/ethereum-launches-2m-quantum-defense-team-as-threat-timeline-accelerates/

data.title: Neynar co-founders released Farcaster's strategic vision and clarified that the client, protocol, and Clanker will not be shut down.
data.description: Neynar co-founder Rish announced on the X platform that he has officially taken over the Farcaster protocol, operating its client and Clanker. His future vision is to continue building a builder-centric ecosystem, enabling creators and developers to smoothly transition from initial ideas to sustainable revenue models. This includes: supporting product openness, ensuring that products built on Farcaster can be used on any platform and directly support global trading, asset issuance, and on-chain ecosystems; ecosystem integration and fusion of software generation, crypto-native payments, and a builder-first network; and supporting multiple types of builders. Rish emphasized that the Farcaster client, protocol, and Clanker will not be shut down, and product iterations will be based on community feedback. Trader wallets and low-fee subscriptions for professional subscribers will also remain unchanged. Short-term planning will begin with priority assessments.

data.title: Neynar's Vision for Farcaster: A Builder-First Network
data.description:
On January 24, Neynar co-founder Rishav Mukherji shared insights on the future of Farcaster. According to BlockBeats, Neynar plans to maintain the Farcaster protocol, operate its client, and manage Clanker. Some Clanker team members will join Neynar, which has been a key infrastructure provider in the Farcaster ecosystem. This integration is seen as a natural progression after years of deep community involvement.

The vision is to enable builders to transition from ideas to sustainable income, creating a 'builder-first' network. Farcaster's core value lies in its builder community, which has driven innovations like Frames, Degen, and Warpcast. The value of Farcaster extends beyond products to those who experiment, share knowledge, and build openly. Neynar's acquisition of Farcaster aims to simplify software development.

There will be no major changes to Farcaster, Neynar, or Clanker products, and rumors about protocol or client shutdowns are unfounded. Neynar's developer platform will continue, and financial and trading functions will remain unchanged. The next phase will focus on product prioritization, advancing the roadmap while maintaining network openness. As software development barriers decrease, combined with crypto-native financial tracks and open distribution mechanisms, Farcaster is poised to become a vital starting point for creators and developers to experiment, collaborate, and scale.

data.title: Bill Miller IV: Despite Bitcoin's failure to hold above $100,000, I remain firmly bullish.
data.description: Bill Miller IV of Miller Value Partners stated that despite Bitcoin's poor performance and failure to hold above $100,000, he remains firmly bullish on Bitcoin. He believes the current pullback is a healthy consolidation and that Bitcoin has formed a higher bottom than it did in early 2025. (Bitcoin News)

data.title: Cardano Faces Challenges in Sustaining Price Momentum
data.description: Cardano's price has struggled to maintain momentum near $0.37, facing repeated setbacks. According to NS3.AI, the cryptocurrency's weak bullish divergence signals, increased profit-taking by holders, and reduced demand from whales have contributed to this trend. Despite a peak in development activity, it has since faltered, leading to intensified selling pressure with each rally attempt. The reduction in whale holdings further exacerbates the situation. For Cardano to achieve a sustained upward trend, it requires stronger development support, slower selling, and renewed whale buying above critical resistance levels.

data.title: TikTok Forms U.S. Entity with Oracle and Key Investors
data.description: TikTok has created a new U.S. entity in collaboration with Oracle, Silver Lake, and MGX as principal investors, ensuring its continued operations in the United States. According to NS3.AI, Oracle's stock experienced a decline following the announcement of the deal, maintaining a recent downward trend despite optimistic price targets from some analysts. The mixed investor sentiment is attributed to challenges in Oracle's GPU cloud business and conservative growth projections ahead of its earnings report.

data.title: Analysis: Strategy's European perpetual stock, STRE, has seen a lackluster market response; its future strategic direction is now in focus.
data.description: Strategy launched its first non-US perpetual preferred stock, STRE, in Europe last November. With a face value of €100 and an annualized dividend of 10%, it was ultimately issued at a discount of €80, raising approximately $715 million. However, market response was lukewarm after listing. Analysts believe the STRE's poor reception was mainly due to limited listing channels on Luxembourg's Euro MTF, difficulty in trading on mainstream brokerages and retail platforms, and a lack of transparent pricing and market data. Strategy has not yet announced its next steps, and the market is focused on whether it will continue to deepen its presence in Europe or maintain its US market focus. (CoinDesk)

data.description:

data.title: Strategy's European Launch of Perpetual Preferred Stock Faces Challenges
data.description:
Strategy introduced its first non-U.S. perpetual preferred stock, STRE, in Europe last November. According to PANews, the stock was issued at a discounted price of 80 euros, despite having a face value of 100 euros and an annual dividend yield of 10%. The launch raised approximately $715 million, but the market response has been tepid.

Analysts attribute the lackluster reception to several factors, including the limited listing channel on Luxembourg's Euro MTF, which restricts trading by mainstream brokers and retail platforms. Additionally, there is a lack of transparent pricing and market data for STRE.

Strategy has not yet announced any future plans, leaving the market to speculate whether the company will continue to focus on the European market or maintain its presence in the U.S. market.

data.title: Ripple CEO Predicts Cryptocurrency Market Surge by 2026
data.description: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse anticipates that cryptocurrency markets, including XRP, will reach new all-time highs by 2026. According to NS3.AI, Garlinghouse attributes this growth to favorable U.S. regulations and increased institutional adoption, with major companies such as BlackRock and Vanguard entering the market. Currently, XRP is consolidating around $1.90, with analysts projecting prices could rise to $8 by 2026, driven by trends in XRP Ledger and Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) adoption.

data.title: Michael Saylor's Firm Introduces European Stock Offering with 10% Dividend
data.description: Michael Saylor's company has introduced Stream, its inaugural non-U.S. perpetual preferred stock, in Europe, offering a 10% dividend. According to NS3.AI, despite the appealing dividend yield, structural challenges in access and market infrastructure have restricted investor adoption. These issues have impeded the anticipated demand from European investors.

data.title: Rick Rieder Emerges as Leading Candidate for Federal Reserve Chair
data.description: Recent data from Polymarket suggests that Rick Rieder holds a 59% probability of being appointed as the next Federal Reserve Chair, making him the leading candidate. According to NS3.AI, other contenders include Kevin Warsh with a 27% chance, Christopher Waller at 6%, and Kevin Hassett at 5%. Rieder's potential appointment is noteworthy due to his views on cryptocurrency, which could impact future Federal Reserve policies affecting the crypto market.
